release_notes: Animation rework: peek-out/peek-in transitions, varied durations, multi-waypoint journeys. Every animation now begins with the stick figure peeking out from beside the Ready button (the player draws a button-shaped mask on top of the figure every frame, so figure parts that overlap the button get visually clipped — matches the reference image of a figure leaning out from behind a vertical pole) and ends with a matching peek-back-in. Durations now vary 7-14s instead of all clustered around 8-12s: Surprised 7s, Cartwheels 8s, Horse 9s, Jumping jacks 10s, Stretches 11s, Lifts weights 11s, Dance 12s, Reads paper 13s, Yoga 13s, Power nap 14s. Each animation now visits 2-3 distinct waypoints on the window instead of going to one generic spot: Stretches does toes / overhead / side-twist at three locations; Dance does side-step / kick-line / spin at three; Yoga does Tree / Warrior II / Mountain at three; Reads-paper sits to read then stands to read at two; Lifts-weights presses at two spots; Horse and Cartwheels traverse the window then return; Sleep stays at one corner but with a much longer nap. Implementation: ready_animations.AnimationPlayer now passes a button info dict (x, y, w, h, color, label) to each draw_fn instead of just button center coords, and draws _draw_button_mask() over the figure each frame to enable the peek effect. New helpers _phase / _walk / _emerge / _hide_back let each animation read as a flat sequence of phase blocks. play_animation pulls the button color, label, and corner radius off the widget at start so the mask matches whatever theme is active. Top hat character is the default look in the picker.
